methodology_sections: 2.3 Data Flow Tracing - Input flow, external data, asset movement, permission escalation
3.2 Business Logic Analysis - Economic incentives, edge cases, assumption violations, race conditions

path: _getUpdatedState() accrues interest and processes expired batches
hooks.onDeposit() external call to hooks contract
transformations: amount -> min(amount, maximumDeposit) caps at max supply
amount -> scaleAmount(amount) = rayDiv(amount, scaleFactor) converts to internal scaled representation
sinks: underlying asset balance of market contract
data_flows.trust_changes: User-supplied amount enters trusted internal accounting after scaling
exposure_risks: Hook callback receives lender address and scaled amount
manipulation_risks: Fee-on-transfer tokens: actual received amount < credited amount, inflating internal accounting
Rebasing tokens: balance changes between scaleAmount calculation and transfer

transformations: For queueFullWithdrawal: scaledAmount = account.scaledBalance, normalizedAmount = normalizeAmount(scaledAmount)
data_flows.trust_changes: User balance moves from active supply to pending withdrawal batch
exposure_risks: Hook receives lender address, expiry, scaled amount
manipulation_risks: Rounding difference between queueWithdrawal (uses scaleAmount on user-provided amount) and queueFullWithdrawal (uses exact scaledBalance) can lead to dust differences

path: executeWithdrawal called with accountAddress and expiry
mulDiv(batch.normalizedAmountPaid, status.scaledAmount, batch.scaledTotalAmount) computes pro-rata share
If sanctioned: create escrow and transfer there
sinks: accountAddress or escrow contract receives underlying tokens
data_flows.trust_changes: Reserved withdrawal funds released to user or escrow
exposure_risks: Anyone can trigger executeWithdrawal for any account, routing sanctioned user funds to escrow
manipulation_risks: Pro-rata rounding leaves dust in batch (acknowledged in code comments)
Sanctioned status can change between queueing and executing, redirecting funds to escrow

data_flows.trust_changes: Protocol assets leave market, increasing delinquency risk
manipulation_risks: Borrower can borrow right up to the reserve ratio limit, leaving no buffer for market fluctuations

path: scaleFactor increases by base interest + delinquency fee
timeDelinquent updated based on current delinquency status
transformations: delinquencyFee applied only when timeDelinquent > gracePeriod
data_flows.trust_changes: Time-based state progression trusted via block.timestamp
validation_points: Two-phase accrual: first to expiry, then to current timestamp
manipulation_risks: Tiny deposits at high frequency to maximize rounding in attacker's favor
Delinquency timer manipulation via strategic repayment just before grace period expires

path: Settle remaining debt: if underfunded, pull from borrower; if excess, return to borrower
data_flows.trust_changes: Market transitions from active to closed state permanently
validation_points: All withdrawal batches must be payable (scaledPendingWithdrawals == 0)
manipulation_risks: CRITICAL: availableLiquidity -= normalizedAmountPaid can underflow due to rounding in scaleAmount (rayDiv rounds to nearest) producing normalizedAmountPaid > availableLiquidity, blocking market closure

path: isSanctioned combines Chainalysis check with borrower override
nukeFromOrbit: queues full withdrawal for sanctioned account
executeWithdrawal: routes funds to escrow if sanctioned at execution time
Escrow holds funds until sanctions cleared or borrower overrides
transformations: Account balance -> queued withdrawal -> escrow-held funds
data_flows.trust_changes: External oracle (Chainalysis) controls fund routing; borrower can override
exposure_risks: Escrow address deterministic via CREATE2 - can be precomputed
manipulation_risks: Borrower can override sanctions for any account, potentially unfreezing sanctioned funds
Sanctions status can change between queue and execute, affecting fund routing

path: updateScaleFactorAndFees calculates protocol fee as fraction of interest
data_flows.trust_changes: Protocol fees have lower priority than unclaimed withdrawals
manipulation_risks: withdrawableProtocolFees can underflow if totalAssets < normalizedUnclaimedWithdrawals

path: Validate hooks instance is from known template
Call IHooks.onCreateMarket to finalize hooks config
Market constructor reads params from factory.getMarketParameters()
transformations: name = namePrefix + asset.name(), symbol = symbolPrefix + asset.symbol()
Strings packed into 2 x bytes32 words
sinks: New market contract deployed at deterministic address
data_flows.trust_changes: Factory is trusted to set market parameters; hooks instance can modify hooks config during onCreateMarket
validation_points: Salt contains sender address or is zero
exposure_risks: Transient storage used for parameter passing - only exists during deployment transaction
manipulation_risks: Hooks onCreateMarket can modify the HooksConfig, potentially disabling important hooks
Name/symbol packed strings limited to 63 bytes total

core_rules: liquidityRequired = normalizeAmount(scaledRequiredReserves) + accruedProtocolFees + normalizedUnclaimedWithdrawals where scaledRequiredReserves = (scaledTotalSupply - scaledPendingWithdrawals) * reserveRatioBips / BIP + scaledPendingWithdrawals
Withdrawal batches expire after withdrawalBatchDuration; FIFO queue for unpaid batches
Market tokens are rebasing: balanceOf = scaledBalance * scaleFactor / RAY
Protocol fees are lower priority than unclaimed withdrawals
economic_incentives: Lenders earn annualInterestBips on deposited assets via increasing scaleFactor
Borrowers pay base interest + potential delinquency fee
Delinquency fee applied when timeDelinquent > delinquencyGracePeriod, incentivizing timely repayment
Protocol earns protocolFeeBips as fraction of interest, extracted from lender yield
Reserve ratio requirement prevents borrower from withdrawing all assets
governance_mechanisms: ArchController owner manages borrower/factory/controller registration and asset blacklist
Borrower controls market parameters (APR, reserve ratio, max supply) with hook-based guardrails
Factory owner can update protocol fees for hooks templates
Protocol fee updates pushed to existing markets via pushProtocolFeeBipsUpdates
Borrower can override Chainalysis sanctions for specific accounts

edge_cases.scenario: closeMarket with dust availableLiquidity and scaleFactor >= 2*RAY causes underflow in `availableLiquidity -= normalizedAmountPaid`
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Market should close successfully after borrower covers all debts
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Reverts due to arithmetic underflow because scaleAmount (rayDiv) rounds up, making normalizedAmountPaid > availableLiquidity. For scaleFactor=2e27, availableLiquidity=1 → normalizedAmountPaid=2.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: A market with scaleFactor >= 2*RAY (100%+ accumulated interest) cannot be closed if dust amounts remain after batch processing. Borrower is stuck with an uncloseable market. The attacker could deliberately create tiny withdrawal batches to trigger this condition. Unlike repayAndProcessUnpaidWithdrawalBatches which uses satSub, closeMarket uses regular subtraction.

edge_cases.scenario: Fee-on-transfer token used as underlying asset
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Deposit of amount X credits user with scaled tokens worth X underlying
edge_cases.actual_behavior: safeTransferFrom(sender, market, X) delivers X-fee to market, but scaledAmount is computed on X, inflating internal accounting. Over time this creates a deficit where total claims exceed actual assets.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Last withdrawers cannot fully withdraw. Attacker deposits with fee-on-transfer token, gets credited full amount, then withdraws more than their fair share. Market becomes insolvent.
collectFees reverts with panic instead of a meaningful error. Not exploitable for fund theft but prevents fee collection and provides poor UX.

edge_cases.scenario: Transfer emits incorrect amount due to scale/normalize rounding
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Transfer event amount matches actual value transferred
edge_cases.actual_behavior: In _transfer, emit_Transfer uses original `amount` but actual value transferred is normalizeAmount(scaleAmount(amount)) which may differ. Similarly _depositUpTo emits `amount` not the normalized value of scaledAmount.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: External trackers (block explorers, portfolio tools) may show incorrect balances. Off-chain accounting discrepancies.

edge_cases.scenario: Allowance consumed exceeds actual transferred value in transferFrom
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Allowance reduced by actual transferred value
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Allowance reduced by `amount` parameter, but actual transfer is normalizeAmount(scaleAmount(amount)) which can be less due to rounding. Approver's allowance is consumed faster than value actually moves.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Minor allowance waste for approvers. Cannot be exploited for profit.

edge_cases.expected_behavior: State written once with final values
edge_cases.actual_behavior: _queueWithdrawal calls _writeState, then nukeFromOrbit calls _writeState again with the same state object. Both recalculate isDelinquent from totalAssets(). Second write overwrites first.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Gas waste only. Not exploitable since totalAssets is unchanged between the two writes.

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Batch expires immediately and is processed in same transaction
edge_cases.actual_behavior: In _getUpdatedState, hasPendingExpiredBatch checks expiry > 0 && block.timestamp > expiry. If expiry == block.timestamp (created in same block), batch is NOT expired yet. Must wait until next block. With duration 0, expiry = block.timestamp, so the batch won't be processed as expired until the next transaction in a later block.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Minor delay in withdrawal processing. Cannot be weaponized.

edge_cases.actual_behavior: satSub returns 0, then _repay reverts with NullRepayAmount
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: UX issue only. Callers must check debt before calling.

edge_cases.scenario: Hooks onSetAnnualInterestAndReserveRatioBips returns different values than borrower requested
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Hook can validate and optionally modify parameters within bounds
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Hook return values masked to uint16 and used directly. Validation checks (> BIP) are applied after hook. Hook could set values to maximum uint16 (65535) but BIP check (10000) catches it.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Hook is trusted by design. If compromised, could set unfavorable rates within BIP bounds.

edge_cases.scenario: processUnpaidWithdrawalBatch processes same batch repeatedly when not fully paid
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Move to next batch after processing
edge_cases.actual_behavior: In closeMarket loop, _processUnpaidWithdrawalBatch always reads first() from unpaidBatches. If batch isn't fully paid, it stays at front. Loop iterates numBatches times but processes same batch each time with decreasing liquidity.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: In closeMarket, combined with the rounding underflow issue, this can prevent market closure. The loop wastes gas processing the same batch. In repayAndProcessUnpaidWithdrawalBatches, the while loop has availableLiquidity > 0 condition that mitigates this.

edge_cases.scenario: rescueTokens called on a malicious ERC20 with reentrant callback
edge_cases.actual_behavior: rescueTokens has no nonReentrant modifier. Malicious token could call back. However, reentrant calls to other market functions are protected by nonReentrant. rescueTokens itself re-entering would transfer 0 on second call.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Limited attack surface since only borrower can call and state-modifying functions are protected.

assumption_violations: Assumes underlying asset is standard ERC20 (no fee-on-transfer, no rebasing, no hooks). Violated by deflationary or rebasing tokens.
Assumes scaleAmount(normalizeAmount(x)) ≈ x with bounded error. Can diverge significantly when scaleFactor >> RAY.
Assumes totalAssets() >= normalizedUnclaimedWithdrawals at all times. Can be violated if underlying asset is deflationary or lost.
Assumes Chainalysis oracle is reliable and available. Oracle failure blocks sanctions-related operations.
Assumes hooks contract is well-behaved and does not consume excessive gas or revert unexpectedly.
Assumes block.timestamp is not manipulable (true for post-Merge Ethereum but may differ on L2s).
game_theory_risks: Borrower can strategically repay just enough to reset delinquency timer before grace period expires, avoiding penalty APR indefinitely while remaining marginally delinquent.
Last-lender problem: when market is nearly empty, remaining lender may face disproportionate rounding losses. The dust from withdrawal batch rounding accumulates as unrecoverable.
Borrower can set very high interest rate and low reserve ratio to attract deposits, then borrow max and default. Only mitigated by hooks-based access control.
Front-running withdrawal batches: a lender seeing a large pending withdrawal could deposit and queue withdrawal to join the same batch, diluting other lenders' share of available liquidity.
Protocol fee recipient has no incentive to maintain market health but benefits from higher interest rates which increase fees.
Borrower override of sanctions creates moral hazard - borrower could unsanction addresses to access frozen funds.
integration_risks: Chainalysis sanctions list dependency: if the oracle address changes or becomes unavailable, all sanctions-related functions will fail
Hook contracts are immutable per market - if a bug is found in hooks, the market cannot be patched
SphereX engine integration adds external call overhead to every guarded function and could be a denial-of-service vector if engine reverts
Factory transient storage pattern for market parameters assumes constructor callback happens atomically - safe in current EVM but relies on transient storage semantics
calculation_errors: In onBorrow hook (HooksConfig.sol), size calculation uses `RepayHook_Base_Size` instead of `BorrowHook_Base_Size`. Both happen to equal 0x0224 currently, but if borrow hook parameters change independently, this would produce wrong calldata size.

closeMarket: `availableLiquidity -= normalizedAmountPaid` does not use satSub, unlike repayAndProcessUnpaidWithdrawalBatches which does. This inconsistency leads to potential revert from rounding-induced underflow.

_applyWithdrawalBatchPayment uses scaleAmount (rayDiv, rounds to nearest) for available liquidity but mulDiv (rounds down) for payment amount. The mismatch means payment can exceed input liquidity when scaleFactor >= 2*RAY.

token_semantics.notes: Rebasing token where balanceOf = scaledBalance * scaleFactor / RAY. Decimals match underlying asset. Transfer triggers hooks and state update. balanceOf is a view that accrues interest. Transfer amount vs scaled amount have rounding discrepancy.
Protocol assumes standard ERC20 behavior. Fee-on-transfer or rebasing tokens will break accounting. SafeERC20 (LibERC20) used for transfers. No received-amount delta checking performed.

invariants.name: supply_conservation
invariants.description: scaledTotalSupply = sum(all scaledBalances including market's own balance from pending withdrawals). scaledPendingWithdrawals <= scaledTotalSupply.

invariants.name: asset_coverage
invariants.description: In healthy state: totalAssets() >= normalizedUnclaimedWithdrawals + accruedProtocolFees. Violated when market is delinquent.

invariants.name: withdrawal_batch_consistency
invariants.description: scaledAmountBurned <= scaledTotalAmount. normalizedAmountPaid monotonically increases. When scaledAmountBurned == scaledTotalAmount, batch is fully paid.

invariants.name: delinquency_tracking
invariants.description: isDelinquent = (liquidityRequired() > totalAssets()). timeDelinquent increases while delinquent, decreases while not. Penalty APR applied when timeDelinquent > delinquencyGracePeriod.

invariants.name: closed_market_rules
invariants.description: When isClosed: annualInterestBips=0, reserveRatioBips=10000, timeDelinquent=0. No new deposits, borrows, or repays. Withdrawals can be executed before expiry.

invariants.name: scale_factor_monotonic
invariants.description: scaleFactor only increases over time (never decreases). Starts at RAY (1e27). Increases via interest accrual and delinquency fees.
